violations.text: Oxtail was observed held at 130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Oxtail was reheated. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the stepstool, rolling rack, and outside edge of food prep sink have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The oil recycling bin has an accumulation of grease and debris. The bin must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
The dumpster & oil recycling bin enclosure has an accumulation of grease and gravel. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
Wall tile near the kitchen door is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was taken to the 3 bay sink to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Cooked vegetables observed held at 74 degrees F on the stovetop. Chicken in the hot hold case observed at 97 degrees F. Chicken was observed held at 94-97 degrees Fahrenheit in the steam table. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All foods were taken to be reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Fish in the walk in cooler was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Employee showed inspector where rodent bait had been strewn on the ground near the dumpster area. Rodent bait shall be contained in a covered, tamper-resistant bait station. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026
Thermometer in the beverage refrigerator is not in good repair, unreadable. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rat droppings observed near the dumpster enclosure. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PCS unknown. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

Beverages were observed stored on the floor on the side of the hand sink in the front service area, directly beneath the paper towel dispenser. Food must be stored in a clean dry location, where it is not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Scoops in containers of dry ingredients observed with the handles in the food. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the basement storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026
Prep unit was not in good repair, nonfunctional during inspection.Prep unit must be repaired, replaced, or removed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink, as the ones on site expired in May 2018. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:ladder in front of hot hold cabinet in service areaprep unitingredient binsrefrigeratorfreezerschest freezerhot hold unitsportable fan in front service areadunnage racks, especially in walk in coolerDelivery plastic observed on metal shelf in the front service area. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026
The receptacle in the employee toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
The handwashing sink in the kitchen is in poor repair, dripping into a container. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen and basement. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer adjusted during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024
Caulking at 2 bay prep sink was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the speed rack have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
Dumpster was observed with lids opened. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

The floors underneath shelving in the basement have an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
2 light bulbs under kitchen hood and 1 light bulb in walk-in cooler were observed burned out. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024
